a_image.alt: Rich Skeuomorphism (Apple Classic) style mood board — Apple Design · iOS 1–6 Era

sections.a_description: Brushed aluminum toolbars, linen textures peeking through dropped panels, and that iconic Apple blue — Rich Skeuomorphism captures the polished, premium tactility of Apple's pre-flat design era. This is the iOS that gave your Newsstand app wooden shelves and your Podcast app a real reel-to-reel tape deck, all rendered with an obsessive attention to material authenticity.

This Apple-era aesthetic appears on design history retrospectives, iOS nostalgia projects, and premium app showcases that want to recapture the tactile richness of early iPhone design. You'll find it on creative tool interfaces, music production apps, and portfolio sites that believe great UI should feel as crafted as the physical objects it represents.

a_image_cover.alt: Rich Skeuomorphism (Apple Classic) style use case illustration
Rich Skeuomorphism (Apple Classic) style use case illustration
Rich Skeuomorphism (Apple Classic) style use case illustration

sections.a_description: Apple's peak skeuomorphic era — the brushed aluminum, linen drawer, and felt game table aesthetic of iOS 1–6 and OS X Lion. Background: fine linen texture in warm gray (#d4cfc7) via a subtle repeating-linear-gradient crosshatch pattern. The .inner panel is a bright white card with generous rounded corners (14px), a crisp 1px border (#b8b8b8), and a refined drop-shadow (0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)). A brushed-aluminum header bar at the top of the card: linear-gradient from #e8e8e8 to #cfcfcf with a 1px bottom border (#b0b0b0) — the classic iOS navigation bar look. Text is crisp dark gray (#1a1a1a) on white. The aesthetic is CLEAN skeuomorphism — not heavy leather, but polished aluminum and glass. Set in Inter — channeling the Helvetica Neue that Apple used across iOS. Heading at weight 700, 28px, letter-spacing -0.5px, line-height 1.2, color #1a1a1a. Eyebrow at weight 500, 12px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #007aff (Apple blue). Body at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.6, color #333. Buttons: primary is the classic iOS blue (#007aff) glossy pill with white text, rounded (8px), gradient from #3d9aff to #007aff, inset white highlight. Secondary: white with thin gray border. Alternatives: SF Pro Display (native Apple), Nunito (rounded), DM Sans (geometric).
